Summary.Heading: Refund Status Inquiry
Summary.Description: Where's My Refund? - Most common call type

Detail.Heading: Refund Status Inquiry Procedures
Detail.Description: Handle taxpayer inquiries about refund status, processing times, and direct deposit issues.

Sections.TemplateString:
🔐 Required Authentication

  • Full SSN or ITIN
  • Filing status from return
  • Exact refund amount claimed
  • Tax year in question

📋 Agent Instructions

  1. Verify identity using IRM 21.1.3.2.3
  2. Access IDRS command code IMFOL
  3. Check TC 846 (refund issued) or TC 840 (manual refund)
  4. If no TC 846: Check for freeze codes (−R, −A, −C)
  5. Provide realistic timeframe based on processing cycle

⏱️ Standard Processing Times

E-filed + Direct Deposit21 days
Paper filed6-8 weeks
Amended (1040-X)16-20 weeks
With EITC/ACTCMid-February (PATH Act)

⚠️ MUST CAPTURE

  • Caller's callback number
  • Best time to reach
  • Confirmation of mailing address
  • Direct deposit info if changed

📚 Policy References

  • IRM 21.4.1 - Refund Research
  • IRM 21.4.2 - Refund Trace
  • Where's My Refund Tool

Detail.Heading: Identity Verification Procedures
Detail.Description: Verify taxpayer identity for those who received Letter 5071C, 5747C, 6331C, or 4883C.

Sections.TemplateString:
🔐 Mandatory Verification Items

  • Letter number (5071C, 5747C, 6331C, 4883C)
  • Control number from letter
  • SSN and date of birth
  • Current mailing address
  • One financial account number (last 4 digits)
  • Information from prior year return

📋 Agent Instructions

  1. NEVER process without completing full ID verification
  2. Access ID Verify application via SERP
  3. Ask knowledge-based questions from system
  4. Document all responses in AMS
  5. If verification fails 2x, require in-person visit
  6. Issue Letter 5747C if online verification unavailable

🚨 FRAUD INDICATORS - STOP & ESCALATE

  • Caller unable to answer basic questions
  • Background noise suggesting call center
  • Rushing agent to complete quickly
  • Address change requested immediately after verification
  • Multiple failed verification attempts same day

⚠️ MUST CAPTURE

  • Letter number and control number
  • All KBA responses (pass/fail)
  • Caller's relationship to taxpayer
  • Any third-party authorization (Form 2848/8821)

📚 Policy References

  • IRM 25.23.2 - Identity Protection
  • IRM 21.1.3 - Authentication
  • Identity Theft Resources

Detail.Heading: Installment Agreement Procedures
Detail.Description: Assist taxpayers with setting up, modifying, or reinstating payment plans for tax liabilities.

Sections.TemplateString:
🔐 Required Information

  • All tax years with balance due
  • Total amount owed (access SUMRY)
  • Monthly income and expenses
  • Bank account info for direct debit
  • Employer name and address

📋 Installment Agreement Types

TypeCriteriaSetup Fee
Guaranteed≤$10,000, filed all returns$31 (DD) / $107
Streamlined≤$50,000, 72-month payoff$31 (DD) / $107
Non-Streamlined>$50,000 or >72 months$107 + F433
PPIACannot full-pay in CSEDRequires F433-A

📋 Agent Instructions

  1. Verify all returns are filed (access TXMODA)
  2. Calculate minimum payment: Balance ÷ months to CSED
  3. For streamlined: Use Online Payment Agreement if eligible
  4. Set up direct debit to reduce user fee
  5. Explain: Miss 2 payments = default + full balance due
  6. Document agreement terms in AMS history

⚠️ MUST CAPTURE

  • All tax years included in agreement
  • Monthly payment amount agreed
  • Payment due date (1st-28th)
  • Bank routing and account number (direct debit)
  • Taxpayer agreement to terms verbally confirmed

📚 Policy References

  • IRM 5.14.1 - Installment Agreements
  • IRM 5.19.1 - Balance Due
  • Online Payment Agreement

Detail.Heading: Transcript Request Procedures
Detail.Description: Process requests for tax return transcripts, account transcripts, and wage/income transcripts.

Sections.TemplateString:
🔐 Authentication Required

  • Full SSN and date of birth
  • Current mailing address on file
  • Tax year(s) requested
  • For third party: Form 4506-T or POA on file

📋 Transcript Types

TypeContainsUse For
Tax ReturnLine items from returnMortgage, student loans
Tax AccountFiling status, payments, adjustmentsAmended return status
Wage & IncomeW-2s, 1099s receivedVerify income, filing
Record of AccountReturn + Account combinedFull tax picture
Verification of Non-FilingConfirms no return filedFinancial aid, benefits

📋 Agent Instructions

  1. First: Direct to IRS.gov/transcripts (instant online)
  2. If online not available: Use Get Transcript by Mail
  3. Phone requests: Order via IDRS or IAT tool
  4. Processing time: 5-10 business days by mail
  5. Wage transcripts available after July 31 for prior year

⚠️ MUST CAPTURE

  • Transcript type requested
  • Tax year(s)
  • Mailing address confirmation
  • Third-party authorization if applicable
  • Reason for request (for notes)

📚 Policy References

  • IRM 21.2.3 - Transcripts
  • Get Transcript Online
  • Form 4506-T Instructions

Detail.Heading: Notice Explanation Procedures
Detail.Description: Explain IRS notices (CP series, Letters) and guide taxpayers on required responses.

Sections.TemplateString:
🔐 Required Information

  • Notice number (CP or LTR)
  • Notice date
  • Tax year referenced
  • Amount due (if applicable)
  • Response deadline

📋 Common Notice Types

NoticeMeaningAction Required
CP14Balance duePay or set up plan
CP2000Income mismatchAgree or dispute
CP501-504Payment remindersPay immediately
LT11Final notice - levyPay or request CDP
CP75EITC auditSubmit documentation
Letter 12CMissing informationSubmit within 20 days

📋 Agent Instructions

  1. Ask for exact notice number (top right corner)
  2. Pull up notice on IDRS (ENMOD, TXMOD)
  3. Explain in plain language what IRS is asking
  4. Calculate response deadline from notice date
  5. Explain consequences of non-response
  6. Offer to set up payment plan if balance due
  7. Document call even if 'information only'

⚠️ MUST CAPTURE

  • Notice number and date
  • Tax year
  • Whether taxpayer agrees or disputes
  • Response deadline communicated
  • Any documentation taxpayer will submit

📚 Policy References

  • IRM 21.3.1 - Notices
  • Understanding Your Notice
  • Collection Process Overview
